title: The Duel Turns Desperate
description: On pages 13–16, this part focuses on the duel turns desperate, keeping the scene centered on the conflict, choices, and stakes of the Alabasta arc.
scene: The Duel Turns Desperate. Nami begins turning Usopp’s flawed invention into a weapon suited to her intelligence and weather sense. This section presents the immediate scene beat before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: These functions are as-yet incomplete so feel free to use them at parties!

dialogue.speaker: Miss Doublefinger
dialogue.text: Then don't write it in the damn manual for God's sake!

dialogue.speaker: Miss Doublefinger
dialogue.text: PLEASE let there be some- thing I can actually use!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I'm sorry, but I can't play with you any longer...

dialogue.speaker: Pell
dialogue.text: I get it! The heat and cold balls are first expelled,

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: And when it stops spinning, they collide, giving birth to an air current,

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: In the form of an explosive gust of wind!

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 2 Bon Clay
dialogue.text: The Cyclone-Tempo is basically a boomerang, making it an ideal toy for when the party heads outdoors!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: The creator himself doesn't even understand it...

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Lastly, the Tornado Tempo is this weapon's ultimate attack!

dialogue.speaker: Alabasta rebel
dialogue.text: As long as your enemy is human, he won't be getting up after taking a hit from this!

dialogue.speaker: Alabasta rebel
dialogue.text: HOWEVER! As it can only be used once, it should be your last resort! Consider yourself doomed if you miss...

summary: This part develops the duel turns desperate and pushes the Alabasta conflict closer to its next decisive turn.
